question.label: Escalation guide
question.name: What would a useful escalate-versus-batch guide need to cover?
question.description: Turns the missing escalation guide into concrete criteria the manager can help provide.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

assessment.note: He names a specific missing context source — practical handoff details only visible in live incidents — which is a strong clarity gap and mild drag on engagement.

new_queue.label: Live incidents
new_queue.name: Which handoff details only become visible during live incidents?

new_queue.name: Who would be most useful for you to spend live time with next?

new_queue.name: What should we line up before our next check-in?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
